title: Argentinian Kid-Friendly Empanada Recipe
description: A fun and easy empanada recipe for kids, inspired by traditional Argentinian cuisine. Perfect for a snack or light meal.

ingredients.name: Kid-Friendly Filling
ingredients.notes: use a mix of shredded cheese and a bit of friendly empanada dough for a fun texture

ingredients.name: Pull apart Argentinian Bread
ingredients.notes: add some crunchy texture to the filling

instructions: 2. Roll out the empanada dough to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
3. Cut out circles of dough using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass.
4. Spoon a small amount of the kid-friendly filling onto the center of each dough circle.
5. Fold the dough in half to form a half-moon shape, and press the edges together with a fork to seal.
6. Brush the tops of the empanadas with a little bit of water and sprinkle with shredded cheese.
7. Bake the empanadas for 15-20 minutes, or until they are golden brown.
